results.evidence: processSubmission calls markVendor(id, vendor.org) at process-submission.ts:271 before publishSkill() at line 285. markVendor writes isVendor=true to KV (submission-store.ts:713-714) and DB (lines 719-721). The call ordering places markVendor before updateState(VENDOR_APPROVED) rather than after as planned, but the requirement is satisfied: isVendor is set before publishSkill runs.

markVendor(id, org) at submission-store.ts:705 reads KV entry, sets isVendor=true, writes back to KV with TTL, then updates DB via prisma.submission.update({data: {isVendor: true, vendorOrg: org}}). DB update is best-effort with try/catch.
markVendor is only called inside the `if (vendor.isVendor)` block at process-submission.ts:270-271. Non-vendor repos take the else path at line 297+.

publishSkill computes effectiveVendor = sub.isVendor || (owner ? isVendorOrg(owner) : false) at submission-store.ts:1022. When effectiveVendor=true: trustScore=100/trustTier=T4 (lines 1026-1028), labels=['vendor','certified'] (line 1067-1068), certTier=CERTIFIED/certMethod=VENDOR_AUTO via deriveCertTier (line 1079).
When sub.isVendor=false and owner is not a vendor org, effectiveVendor=false. Normal trust computation runs (lines 1029-1051). Labels assigned as ['community', 'verified'] (line 1069).
When sub.isVendor=true, effectiveVendor=true via short-circuit OR (line 1022). Same vendor path executes, preserving existing behavior.

No dedicated backfill-vendor-cert endpoint was created (T-004 not implemented as specified). However, the pre-existing trusted-author-backfill endpoint at src/app/api/v1/admin/trusted-author-backfill/route.ts performs the same function: queries vendor-author skills with certTier != CERTIFIED, calls updateSkillTrust() which assigns CERTIFIED/VENDOR_AUTO/T4/100. Functionally equivalent.
The trusted-author-backfill WHERE clause filters certTier != CERTIFIED and trustScore < 100. Already-certified skills are excluded. Second invocation returns updated=0 (idempotent).

findings.title: No dedicated unit tests for markVendor()
findings.description: T-001 specified test file src/lib/__tests__/submission-store.markVendor.test.ts with 4 test cases (KV update, DB update, idempotency, DB-failure resilience). This file does not exist. markVendor has zero direct unit tests.
findings.recommendation: Write the 4 specified test cases: markVendor_setsIsVendorInKV, markVendor_updatesDB, markVendor_isIdempotent, markVendor_DBFailureDoesNotThrow.

findings.title: No unit tests for publishSkill vendor fallback
findings.description: T-003 specified test file src/lib/__tests__/submission-store.publishSkill.test.ts with 3 test cases verifying the effectiveVendor fallback logic. This file does not exist. The defense-in-depth fallback (the most architecturally significant change) has no test coverage.
findings.recommendation: Write the 3 specified test cases: vendorFlagFalseButVendorOwner, vendorFlagFalseNonVendorOwner, vendorFlagTrue_existingPathPreserved.

findings.title: Existing vendor test does not assert markVendor is called
findings.description: The vendor path test at process-submission.test.ts:166-176 asserts updateState(VENDOR_APPROVED), updateState(PUBLISHED), and publishSkill are called, but does NOT assert mockMarkVendor was called. The mock is wired (line 47) but never verified. Additionally, the mock uses { isVendor: true, orgName: 'trusted-org' } but the real checkVendorRepo returns { org: ... } not { orgName: ... }, meaning vendor.org resolves to undefined in the test, silently falling back to 'unknown'.
findings.recommendation: Add expect(mockMarkVendor).toHaveBeenCalledWith('sub-test-1', 'trusted-org'). Fix mock return to use { isVendor: true, org: 'trusted-org' } to match the real checkVendorRepo signature.

findings.title: T-004 backfill endpoint not created as specified
findings.description: tasks.md T-004 specified creating src/app/api/v1/admin/backfill-vendor-cert/route.ts with its own test file. Neither was created. The increment relies on the pre-existing trusted-author-backfill endpoint instead. While functionally equivalent (AC-US3-01/02 are satisfied), the task was marked [x] completed without implementing the specified deliverable.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable if deliberate reuse of existing endpoint. Document the decision in spec.md or tasks.md to explain why a separate endpoint was unnecessary.
